parameters resolution measured in ks and sensor size in film we basically have the physical size of the film as the equivalent of sensor size the bigger the piece of film the more detail there is on it compared to the amount of noise grain and the less magnification is required to fit it on a screen of any given size the types of film guages we have two basic guages for motion picture exhibition 35mm and 65mm but they come in a couple of formats to understand the difference between these formats take our your phone open youtube and watch a video thats in widescreen see how the picture area is a certain size right now flip over to landscape mode suddenly the picture area got a lot bigger didnt it well film is exactly like that a strip of super35mm is 24mm wide but if we flip it on its side we get vistavision which is 36mm across although its normally cropped to 3394mm same with 65mm the standard size whats called 65mm 5perf is 4856mm these formats have different aspect ratios super35mm is between 2391 or 2411 depending on exactly how its cropped 65mm is 221 and imax is around 141 again depending on how its cropped there are other formats like super technirama or fourstrip cinerama but theyre long obsolete the way its shown in theatres now this is all how the formats are captured in terms of how they are projected thats a complicated story most movies today even when captured on film go through a digital master which usually involves funnelling them down to 4k then theyre either projected digitally at 2k or 4k or printed back to film projection either 35mm 65mm or 70mm imax since formats like 70mm imax resolve more than 4k quite how much more ill show later this digital intermediate represents a loss a picture quality and so a few filmmakers like christopher nolan bypass it and do the whole process on film but that isnt to say that what you see in the theatre when mastered on film is the equivalent of what the imax negative looks like mastering on film still comes with inevitably loses because you have to make a print which suffers whats called generational loss compared to the negative its a picture of a picture basically just how big the generational loss is depends on the particularities of the process it ranges from roughly 15 contact print off the negative to 45 the full ipin process to add to the confusion imax is not just a type of film format its also a brand name and so various digital cameras and digital projectors are licensed as imax even though theyre not 70mm imax the addition of 3d andor highframe rate also affect our perception of sharpness but we wont go into that here the equivalent digital formats so how does all of this compare to digital formats well as i said digital formats have sensor size and resolution figures independently of each other bigger sensor makes it easier to get a cleaner image so the alexa 65 looks a lot a fair bit better than the red dragon even though resolutionwise theres only a 05k difference between them at the same time we neednt make too much out of sensor size in and of itself either digital footage is easier to magnify than film without drawing too much attention to the magnification because its a lot cleaner on film the grains become magnified along with the image and if the piece of film is really small compared to the screen the audience becomes acutely aware that theyre watching something 16mm across thats been magnified to the size of three stories its why 16mm film cannot be blownup to imax but footage from a 1080p digital camera can so we all hear these stories about how imax is 18k and 35mm is 6k those stories im afraid confuse the actual resolution with the scanning resolution to scan a piece of film to digital with sharp grains and without having aliasing it must be oversampled at a higher resolution in theory we have to scan at x25 the resolution which is why a 35mm project like the wizard of oz was scanned at 8k but that doesnt mean it resolves nearly as much in fact even scanning at twice the resolution is scarcely needed in practice since our subjects especially in film rarely have that grid pattern that generates a lot of moire typically 35mm is scanned at 4k or sometimes 6k the latter usually for anamorphic wideshots super technirama projects like spartacus are typically scanned at 6k 65mm from lawrence of arabia to nolans own films are scanned at 8k and imax is scanned at 12k another obvious example for why film guages dont resolve such high resolution figures and there are even more hyperbolic figures thrown around as high as 14k for 35mm is a very simple one if 35mm resolved 6k than back in 2006 when a 1080p film like apocalypto was showing in theatres it surely will have looked very poor to our eyes and the fact of the matter is that it doesnt and still doesnt even compared to 4k masters of 35mm projects another example from nolan himself to make sure the vfx dont look pixelated in imax nolan has them done at resolutions above 4k but not as much as you may think for the dark knight they did a handful at 8k but going forward they did them at 56k and recently at 64k so that by itself would suggest the resolution figure for the imax footage are nowhere near as high as those hyperbolic figures no real resolution figures are decidedly more modest according to film technology in postproduction it is estimated that a wellexposed finegrained 35mm negative of a focus chart can resolve the equivalent of about 3000 pixels across the frame to sample an image adequately the scanning resolution must be somewhat finer and according to the visual effects society handbook actual measured values are used for the calculation here the mtf of a 35mm negative scanned with 4k contains only little more than 56lpmm the equivalent of 3k with the image width of super35mm according to the red company 4k scan of fast film 2829k 4k scan of slow film 3132k vistavision 4k so if a super35mm negative which is 2496mm across the line resolves 32k that then means that vistavision at 3394mm resolves 32k x 136 43k consistent with the figure by red 65mm at 4856mm resolves 32k x 194 62k imax at 696mm resolves 32k x 278 89k now there are a series of compounding factors here first the larger formats 65mm and imax have lensing loses largeformat lenses dont achieve the same mtf levels as the best 35mm cine lenses and so they take significant resolution hits due to lens abberation judder dye bleed and other acquisition issues compound the matter especially for imax but further complicating the issue on the digital side a 4k digital camera doesnt actually resolve a full 4k sure it creates 4k files but it would do so even if one were shooting outoffocus rather one ought to treat the resolution figures as a drawer size and its one the camera ultimately can never fill digital cameras have filters necessary for the function of the sensor that eat away some of the resolution theres an optical lowpass filter that softens the image a bit to remove aliasing and if youre shooting in colour a demosaic that loses some more resolution again to avoid aliasing the net loses are sometimes exaggerated at 50 but in pratice they range between 10 and 20 so if 35mm resolves 32k in theory one would need a 4k digital camera to match that as we get into digital cameras with larger sensors like the alexa 65 were also looking at lens abberations similar to a 65mm film camera ontop of it all and on top of all of that we percieve footage differently as stills than we do in motion because grains are distributed randomly across each frame our eyes average more detail across every couple of adjacent frames than is present on any one frame however the same effect also exists on digital camera thanks to the effects of the debayer filter and it exists in either case when the shot is not completely static since it diminishes as the relative size of the grain decreases its a far less pronoucned effect on 65mm and imax than on 35mm and thus doesnt really impact much in the kinds of formats were talking about there are other sideeffects like a certain level of photochemical noise grain giving the illusion of added sharpness and we havent even touched upon anamorphic formats where theres added lens abberation but also a slight magnifying effect from the process so after going through all this many smaller digital cameras but if anything the alexa 65 seems to have a clear edge over the imax here in spite of not just filtering loses but also lens abberations that in turn would put both 65mm glob it makes a lot of sense to the criticism that the man of steel tried to make itself the next version of the dark knight trilogy considering that superman is always known to be a more optimistic hero and a beacon of hope however judging by the films narrative it also makes that this superman was more broody confused and lost considering that he was not superman yet at the very beginning and he was still discovering his powers and purpose it would make sense that superman would feel so lost and figuratively an alien to the rest of the world because knowing that he is the only one on the planet who is kryptonian and has so much power that he does not know how to use it it makes sense that superman had to go through a journey of selfdiscovery and selfreflection sure his earth parents were more broody than they are usually depicted but perhaps this portrayal made sense when the narrative took place because they were probably just as confused and scared of what might happen to superman if people discovered who he was and yes the amount of destruction and the paradoxical jesus symbolism towards superman were obvious and probably unfitting but the destruction made sense considering that he was still getting used to his powers hence this was the plot point for the batman v superman film even though it was poorly directed and the jesus symbolism is also a central feature about superman because his powers makes him a god but he chooses to use his powers for the decency of mankind and to be a symbol of hope thanks to his upbringing and his reflection towards earth so will the new superman film be the same maybe there is obviously a lot of portrayals about superman being more optimistic and kind but i think that i understood that superman had to have his heros journey because he needed to discover himself before becoming superman i was recently rewatching the sam raimi spider man trilogy as well as the old xmen movies and i realise that the conclusion that i came to is somewhat influenced by nostalgia but i genuinely think those movies had more to offer than the recent entries in the genre do the first spiderman and xmen movies are very basic but they work fine at setting up the origins of the characters a movie like this couldnt be made these days nor do i think it would work because superhero origin stories are played out the sequels however which are spiderman 2 and x2 are very good movies that up the stakes and have a resounding emotional impact the great thing about them is that they can also serve as standalone movies someone could watch either of these sequels and find enjoyment in them without having seen the first instalment the third movies in each franchise werent as good xmen last stand is not a movie that i can enjoy a lot but it has some decent moments as despicable as brian singer is his absence probably hurt the final instalment of the trilogy on the other hand sam raimi did direct the third spiderman movie and whilst i think that the film was a bit of a mess and couldve been much better its still something that i can somewhat enjoy if i had to choose between watching spiderman 3 or either of the first two marvel spiderman movies i would certainly pick the former the third marvel spiderman entry no way home is a great spectacle movie but it heavily relies on the viewer having seen all the previous spiderman films and preferably most marvel movies too i certainly dont have the urge to revisit it again like i do the first two raimi movies the crux of the matter lies in the episodic nature of marvel i enjoyed mostly everything leading up to endgame and that movie was a great culmination of the saga but every movie except maybe the first iron man feels like an episode of a tv show that is designed to set up the next stage these movies as great as some of them were to watch at the time dont have as much rewatch value i personally never felt like revisiting either endgame or infinity war since they came out in cinemas rewatching them would sort of feel like watching the last episode of the sopranos or breaking bad on the other hand i have a great urge to rewatch superhero movies that feel like their own standalone story of course the peak of the genre at least to me was the dark knight which can be considered a great thriller movie that transcends superhero tropes but even batman begins is in my opinion a very complete movie that i love revisiting i am not a fan of the dark knight rises and can level a lot of criticism at it but i cant fault it for not feeling like a complete allusions i got during its 3 hours one was chalomet looking a lot like elijah wood at times paul being pulled towards the dark side of power is obviously all a bit frodo too weve kinda been here before right but i also kept getting life of brian vibes and not in a good way with all the hes the messiah stuff which i realise was meant to come off kinda funny and add some muchneeded light relief but just came off flat and tonally jarring to me the whole living with the fremen section felt listless in general as if we had to do it no new characters of any really substance and no feeling of why we fight the biggest crime for me though was how plodding and narratively choppy it all was felt no chemistry between chalomet and zendaya whatsoever and their relationship didnt feel at all realistic at any point meaning that their denouement had no emotional impact whatsoever when he sidelines her paul spends the whole of the last film and half of this one claiming hes utterly against assuming leadership and then abruptly changes his mind completely so quickly upon arriving in the south zendaya hasnt even arrived yet and hes running around shouting im mobb deep the pub quiz anorak fuck the decepticons butlers character is utterly confusing and totally wasted hes suddenly introduced out of nowhere as another pub quiz anorak half way through is a barely sketched out spoiled psycho nepo baby who licks knife blades and kills lieutenants for no good reason damn are the harkonens dumb and they kill their pmcs like cattle and is then dispatched fairly easily in what a surprise another climatic knife fight with fake out hes lost no he hasnt twist finish walken is also completely miscast and utterly wasted as the emperor it felt like he was only there so certain audience members could exclaim walken yes near the start his character is also another moron villain who does exactly what paul needs him to do by needlessly going all the way to arakkis to trigger our everyones here ending and that gurney fella felt like a completely different character from the first one basicall a kind of boomer idiot semicomic bafoon now not withstanding his oh i simply escaped and now im a smuggler hello story riiiiight also villeneuve has become such a compulsive stylistic magpie scott lean even bergman for his styleoversubstance and bizarrely inconsistent geidi prime bw scenes that hes begun stealing from himself arrival blade runner 2049 and even enemy were all heavily referenced but there were no brand new wow ive never seen this before moments that films like this require hoth helms deep the trex set piece in jurassic park etc just lots of desert and very big things and very loud music i dont want to sound like i hated it because i definitely didnt i just thought it was a very pretty pretty mid big scifi blockbuster to showcase a bunch of young attractive actors who have been designated the new movie stars its technically well put together and unfolds with gusto on the imax screen its the movies being the movies and im all for that but its very very far from the cinematic masterpiece the socials whirl is currently proclaiming it and once the more measured takes and outright takedowns start to emerge i feel the tide will start to turn the backlash will build and itll all descend into the standard stans vs haters bullshit that all big blockbuster online discourse eventually sinks to with the recentish decline in marvels slavish fandom and nolan now making big bw historical biogs about physicists the superheroes scifi and swords and sorcery high school crowd have been a little starved of teenage kicks to obsess and fight over barbie fandom just ran out of steam because it had no daft lore or epic moments to endlessly scream and argue about it was just an nrp summer holiday hit no one really cares anymore with dune 2 great title btw its lotrs star wars marvel dark knight time all over again argue about lore tell people they dont get certain elements focus on simple plot points and sledgehammerheavy messaging as if theyre subtle subtexts endlessly mine for factoids and tidbits to share for internet points basically treat the whole thing like some kind of weird competition where the winner is who likes it or hates it the most feel like most people will simply get caught up in the marketing hype buy their tickets get all excited then later on reflection wonder what all the fuss was about and eventually mildly grumble about it online and pretend they never really liked it in the first place after reading snarky takes and passing them off as their own theyll then watch it again when it comes to streaming and thats when mainstream consensus will shift to its actually not that good the first one was better from a marketing perspective though job well and truly done money banked moving on overall i genuinely feel it got delayed because it needed fixing in the editing room because it wasnt hanging together they couldnt do it so moved the release to a graveyard slot end of feb and marketed the hell out of it got the super fans in early to spread word of mouth and before anyone realises its not great its too late its a pretty classic strategy and to be fair maybe the writers turmoil also affected it because the dialogue was awful for my duke and the narrative was both heavy handed and clumsy ill still turn up for dune messiah though because i like movies and it could potentially be the best one one of the bigger arguments surrounding barbie whether it is genuine in its politicsmessage or if it was clever attempt by mattel to rebrand barbie for the current age this isnt uncommon the authenticity and sincerity of the politics of many other blockbusters have been called into question like black panther and the batman however for some blockbusters its rarely a matter of contention fury road dark knight batman v superman and the last jedi and avatar people may dislike these films and even disagree with their politics but rarely is their sincerity questioned their ideas are debated but not whether they were curated by the studios committies and marketing guys so what differentiates the former from the latter batman and batman returns dont get touched keaton is the best dark knight and i will die on that hill not exactly sure why but tim didnt direct the third entry and thusly we lost keaton and got val kilmer and joel schumacher obviously the tone shifted dramatically from dark to cartoonish i personally like forever mainly due to the villainous takes of jim carrey and tlj carrey especially carries the movie when he is on screen its by far not the best but not the worst batman movie anyone with a pop culture acumen knows the 97 entry is abominable and i like it only because its part of the saga batman is a comic but after the gritty on screen portrayal the darker tone is preferable cue the batbutts and ice puns i heard patrick stewart was possibly gonna play freeze and i would have loved to see that angle arnold is the man but seriously yikes and george clooney double yikes although that flash cameo had me rolling uma is fine af after the nolan trilogy these movies kinda get left at the wayside just curious on your opinions movies that are highly acclaimed by everyone and called masterpiece but you find them overrated not bad by any means just overrated and undeserving of the spot and recognition they got for me its the dark knight usual story youre stranded with enough food and water to last you until some undetermined amount of time passes and youre rescued what two movies are you picking some options i thought of option a die hard the dark knight option b oppenheimer reservoir dogs option c a new hope silence of the lambs option d dune part two there was no clash by day which is a horrible name by the way nor have any name characters in this film appeared in previous movies and yet to me this is a sequel it takes the femme fatale archetype here personified by the fatale herself barbara stanwyck and shows what happens to a villainous character after a noir ends how life goes on for her stanwyck plays mae a woman who long ago ditched the small fishing village where she grew up to make her fortune in the big city it did not go well she vaguely talks about a politician she may or may not have fallen in love with but now shes returned to her small town she is courted by the sweet steady jerry paul douglas and the two get married and have a child but jerrys friend earl robert ryan has his eyes set on mae from the moment they meet earl is a horrible human being and senses that mae is not a saint when he first tries to kiss her he says dont kid me baby i know a bottle by the label though mae resists she ultimately begins an affair with earl that threatens to destroy jerry alfred hayes adapting a play by cliford odets who wrote sweet smell of success has crafted mae into one of the most multidimensional female characters in noir something bad happened in her previous life something that she was the cause of and though she got out of it without bullet holes it destroyed her psyche in many ways she hates the town telling her new friend peggy marilyn monroe home is where you come when you run out of places she likes jerry but oftentimes the conversations she has with him amount to her trying to make peace with the fact that this is her life now when jerry proposes she tries to talk him out of it saying shed be bad for him and when shes knee deep in the affair with earl and jerry finds out she has that classic femme beloved rite of passage in film history is for a movie to become what i call a vindicated classic a film that initially received mixed reviews or underperformed before receiving a positive reappraisal citizen kane 1941 blade runner 1982 the thing 1982 etc but what about films that havent had the same luck films that were polarizing then and now to where they havent found a consistent fanbase in his review of the critically panned film stay chris stuckmann mentioned that two factors helping divisive or underperforming films back then were the home video market and the absence of sites that publicly cemented critics views ie rotten tomatoes metacritic films thought of as vindicated classics typically range from the silent era through the 2000s shortly before home video was followed by streaming and the social media boom those factors have made it so a films reputation is usually ingrained quickly and remains the same or only fluctuates slightly this makes it harder for divisive films to escape reputational limbo one example i can think of is dragged across concrete 2019 while im not a fan i noticed how its temporary viewership on netflix led to new reviews yet it hasnt received a major reappraisal and it understandably remains controversial some polarizing films like david lynchs dune alien 3 cloud atlas and mother have many genuine fans but they still havent received a reappraisal significant enough to become classics others like only god forgives the star wars prequel trilogy the last jedi spiderman 3 prometheus the dark knight rises and no time to die are so heavily contested that even a lot of viewers who enjoy them will acknowledge theyre not necessarily great a few recently vindicated classics that come to mind are batman returns jennifers body trilogy has been analyzed through countless lenses political allegory the dark knight as post911 ethics mythic structure bruce wayne as the jungian archetype and genre evolution the trilogy as a legitimization of comicbook cinema these are all valid and insightfulbut also incomplete its entirely possible that others have arrived at similar conclusions or even this precise interpretation in fragmentsbut this post reflects my own synthesis and angle integrating those prior insights into what i believe is a coherent structural arc this post offers a synthesis a reading of the trilogy not as a superhero saga but as a philosophical descent from myth into framework nolans real arc isnt about batmans journeyits about bruce wayne stepping out of the decision loop transferring symbolic weight to structure and rejecting saviorism in favor of systems that survive without him by the dark knight rises bruce has become not a martyr not a tragic hero but something more rare an architect of symbolic succession he repairs the bats autopilot in secret creating a false narrative of sacrificial death he passes the batcave to blake with no instruction or demandjust trust he funds the orphanage quietly redirecting his resources from vigilante action to societal structure and he lets go of the batman identity not in failure but in deliberate closure this is not a heros death its an intentional withdrawal from dependency batman was never meant to be permanent bruce realizes this only at the end that gotham cannot be saved by a man only stabilized by an idea he moves from savior to steward from force to framework the oftdebated final scenewith alfred seeing bruce in the caféis not ambiguous nolan himself has clarified it was meant to be real but more importantly its thematically necessary bruce lives not to continue intervening but to finally not be needed the symbol survives but the man steps away its a radical message baked into the heart of a blockbuster trilogy this principle doesnt just apply to gothamit mirrors the ethos of science and academic publishing itself in these fields what matters is not the prominence of the individual but the durability and replicability of the framework they leave behind just as bruce steps away to let the idea of batman persist without him so too do scientific and intellectual traditions thrive when knowledge systems are built to endure beyond any single contributor this themeof stepping away to let the framework leadechoes even more clearly in nolans later work oppenheimer there too we see a central figure grappling not just with the burden of creation but with the responsibility of relinquishing control once the system is in motion the scientist as reluctant myth the institution as legacy and the idea as the thing that ultimately persists nolan returns to the same philosophical terrain but this time with fission instead of fear and institutions instead of symbols and now with his next confirmed project being an adaptation of homers odyssey we already know the thematic terrain hes returning to legacy journey and homecomingnot as resolution but as reintegration the odyssey too is not about conquest but about relinquishing identity to return to a society that must function without your constant heroism nolan appears to be tracing a throughline that asks not what makes a great person but rather what does a great person leave behind viewed this way the dark knight rises is not about escalation but exit not about batmans endurance but about his obsolescence by design this trilogy isnt a tale of power won or lostits a meditation on leadership symbolic burden and the rarest of acts relinquishing control once the framework is strong enough and remarkably this interpretation coexists with all others it doesnt negate readings about surveillance ethics revolutionary theater or moral ambiguityit contextualizes them each film in the trilogy poses a question about power batman begins what if fear could be weaponized for good the dark knight what if order must be upheld by unethical means the dark knight rises what if the symbol must be relinquished for the system to heal thats not a superhero arc thats a structural handoff acknowledgment this conceptual synthesis emerged through a longform recursive dialogue between myself and chatgptused not as a passive answer engine but as a collaborative reasoning partner the ideas are mine but were refined through sustained interrogation challenge and iterative analysis i include this not to dilute authorship but to reflect the evolving methods by which complex insights are forged in tandem with new tools the process of arriving at the analysisiterative selfreferential and structuredrivenalso mirrors how nolan himself constructs his films layered recursion thematic echo and symbolic architecture are not just elements of his storytelling but tools of his thought in this sense the method and the subject quietly align curious to hear others thoughtsespecially how this framing might reshape your view of bruces final act and whether you see other films attempting a similar structural movie to nail bruce wayne as a believable character however in my opinion the dark knight continues this in a more complex continually satisfying way the only difference being bruce is now the lead of an ensemble that element to me feels very much in line with the major graphic novels that inspired it like year one and the long halloween which tend to look at gotham from a variety of characters viewpoints on the complete other end of the spectrum even very recently lve seen online posts stating joker is actually the main character in the film and i just dont see how you could possibly think they all have something to say ultimately about batman and his mission to save gotham good evil light dark god satan man animal life antilife order chaos batman joker two sides of a coin one untouched and pure the other burned and corroding but still of the same material christopher nolan is visualizing this with a gotham slowly being turned into something primal or elemental all of its people unknowingly drafted into a war of ideology this is a furthering of the sociological aspects of gotham that were a target of the league of shadows in the first film almost as if ras al ghul knew that eventually something like what happens in the second would occur it also is an expansion on the dichotomy shown between batman and joker within alan moores the killing joke which to me the dark knight is the definitive adaptation of gotham is a constantly balancing act between order and chaos spearheaded by batman and joker as opposing figureheads while the scales are flipped and tested with the people of gotham caught in the middle and for the first time on a magnifying lens the film takes great measures in nearly every aspect to separate itself from previous entries in the franchise a lot of elements of the comic books here either are absent or are unrecognizable but its completely by design apart from the film visually having a stripped down sleek aesthetic that sets it apart within the genre the film itself showcases a batman and gotham under metaphorphosis wayne manor has been burned down and bruce now lives in a high rise apartment overlooking the city he protects at night almost as if he wants to view the fruits of his labor even during his brief free time the batcave is no longer a literal cave but a brightly lit corporate warehouse where batman maintains his operations until wayne manor is rebuilt the criminals of the city have been driven into hiding barely holding onto what they have when out of desperation they turn to a man they dont fully understand the joker a actual manifestation of the dark corrupt side of gotham evolving meanwhile the gotham city heroes the elite sit on their thrones continuing to consolidate power even ignoring the corrupt cracks in their foundation though ultimately batman as the hero overcomes his own possible corruption this is the complete opposite of where the previous film starts with the criminals of gotham running the city and the good people unable to do anything about it until they themselves turn to the man they dont fully understand the first stage of this metamorphosis this physical transformation of the world established in batman begins is also showcased by the visual look of the city itself in the first film gotham is depicted as dirty and grungy not so much gothic as in the tim burton films but rather especially as the film travelled to the darkest corner of gotham called the narrows ringing closer to a slum a place that would be an actual nightmare to live in on the actual streets the entire frame looks wet with rain and grime and theres a sense of almost postapocalyptic desperation there are high rise almost futuristic looking skyscrapers but you also get a subtle sense of class disparity where a lot of the actual wealthy elite aside from the waynes and other good eggs are somewhat aloof and unconcerned with the actual desperate struggle of the people literally below them nothing like real life of course its a visually unique depiction of gotham that feels accurate to the source while straying from the gothic art deco so heavily featured in the previous films and batman the animated series in most modern incarnations of batmans mythology gotham is depicted as a living breathing hellhole of constant misery occasionally chewing up and spitting out its citizens into twisted homicidal funhouse mirrors of batman reflecting the cyclical ultimately sad nature of his undying mission often i see fans criticize the dark knight for gotham just looking like chicago nolan does use chicago extensively for gotham in the film and i understand the disappointment of gotham city not being fully accurate to its typical appearance in the most high profile critically acclaimed piece of batman media maybe ever however i think these fans might be simplifying or even perhaps missing out on the uniquely cinematic and thematically relevant reinvention of gotham city portrayed in nolans films christopher nolan is smart enough to know in time his films wont be considered the one true definitive take on batman but instead simply christopher nolans batman and uses every tool in his cinematic toolbox to tie this unique incarnation of the mythology together in a neat bow the dark knight was filmed primarily in chicago which indeed is quite apparent but ld also like to point out that batman begins was primarily shot in london with the exception of a few scenes shot in chicago quite notably the tumbler chase scene which in the film is the first public appearance of the batman on a comic book page batmans mission truly can be undying as it lives through words and illustrated images on a static page in a film however like in life if the story continues it must continue to evolve the choice to use an architecturally modern brightly lit metropolis to portray the same city that was just a film before depicted as a grimy hole of urban oldworld decay is not an accident gotham city in both a metaphorical and literal sense transforms before our very eyes as do its characters putting on literal masks to become physical manifestations of symbols and ideas in the dark knight the scales have been tipped the city pulling itself from the gutter of absolute desperation and itself having put on a mask of architectural magnificence and artificial clinical light batman uses far more advanced technology here than in any previous batman film to the point where it feels at times closer to science fiction which is reflected in batman surrounding himself at home and his makeshift cave in a stripped down modern minimalism that feels straight out of 2001 a space odyssey he has transformed beyond the dire gothic nature of his start and has started to genuinely dramatically change things within the city batman and gotham both wear a mask but the true face of the city itself hides true darkness pure immature latent chaos that has been totally suppressed by those committed to complete order as such the joker is almost defined by his almost animalistic savagery and commitment to social anarchy look what i did with a few drums of gas and a couple of bullets no advanced technology just gas and bullets still done with the same minimalistic approach as batman but only because the film itself has gotham stripped of all its fat and displaying its essential elements in pure form the joker is birthed of gothams ideological war a necessary piece of the puzzle in this sense he is not truly a man but a symbol exactly bruces initial stated ambition for becoming batman the joker is acutely aware of his place in this psychological conflict showing it with a strange fascination and unnerving kinship to batman during the interrogation but also with the multiple origins he crafts for himself which really display different ways the human psyche can be broken down by one bad day all of those origins are true but not at
